compatibility_report:
Elden Ring:  EAC fails to load, gives Launch Error:  "Unexpected Error:  #1"

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/7452.
@ConHuevosGuey posted on 2024-01-28T15:30:24:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:  Elden Ring
- Steam AppID of the game:

## System Information
- Distrobution:  Slackware 15.0
- GPU:  Nvidia RTX 3090ti
- Video driver version: Nvidia 545.29.06
- Kernel version: 6.1.66
- Proton version:  Proton Experimental (Tried all available versions as well)

## I confirm:
- [X ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1245620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14076553/steam-1245620.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When launching the game the EAC popup occurs, attempts to load then has the title "Launch Error"  with "Unexpected Error: #1" and the game must exit.

## Reproduction
Launch the game.

##  Comments

I can find no other error logs as to why EAC keeps faiiling when loading.  If I use the flatpak version of steam, the game loads fine and EAC loads correctly and I can play online using Steam's flatpak version.  The game ran perfectly fine, including loading EAC when glibc 2.33 was installed.  However after updating glibc I continued to keep getting error #1 on the EAC loading banner.  I've confirmed with others that the game does indeed work with my current version of glibc (2.38), however they are Arch and Nobara distrobutions.

I'm unsure what the cause of this issue as I cannot find any information on error #1 or how to further troubleshoot it.  Nothing appears to fail in the logs and it simply seems to be the EAC module failing to load correctly for some reason. posted at

following_discussion.discussion_body:
**EDIT: Resolved. Be careful with 3rd party KWin plugins ("cornershader")**
I have an issue with the game crashing to desktop shortly after launching, but at slightly varying times. I can make it to the main menu menu at best. When that happens, it also crashes KWin, which closes all open applications. According to the log, it does also crash Xwayland, but I can't tell whether that's happening first or if XWayland simply gets terminated due to Kwin crashing…
dmesg does not indicate that the GPU (driver) crashed or was reset.

Gentoo Linux w/ glibc 2.38r10
Kernel 6.7.1 & 6.7.3
5900X, Radeon 6800XT
Mesa 23.3.5 and 24.0.0
KDE Plasma 5.27.10, Wayland

I tried Proton 8-5, GE-Proton8-22 (both of these worked for me before), Proton experimental, and GE-Proton8-30
Launch parameters I tried: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0

I already recompiled mesa, xwayland (23.2.4) and libX11 (1.8.7), but to no avail. Do you have any ideas on what I could try next? Does the log give a hint as to what exactly is causing the crash?
[steam-1245620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14154447/steam-1245620.log)

@dsalt I found out, what the cause was: It was the "RoundedSBE" window decoration, which comes with a shader/effect plugin for KWin. The plugin is responsible for rounding window corners and *should* deactivate itself for maximized windows and fullscreen applications.

Somehow, due to whatever obscure reason, it suddenly decided to not do that for Elden Ring anymore and crash KWin instead, while other games, like Remnant II, native titles and various console emulators continued to work fine. I could fully resolve the issue by deactivating the so called "cornershader" and using "lightlyshaders" instead.

Recently I've been having Elder Ring crashing constantly since the last 2 weeks and causing my Steam Deck to fully freeze, needing to reboot it.
This happens with the current stable Proton 8.0-5, Experimental and Bleeding Edge, even with the most recent ProtonGEs.
Currently the only version that works perfectly is ProtonGE 8-25, never had any crashes or freezes with it.
I've attached the log of the crash.
[steam-1245620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14190331/steam-1245620.log)
@Nutjob do the crashes happen in a specific location? I haven't been able to reproduce on my LCD deck so far.

@runar-work Usually they happen within 10-20 minutes, 40 at the latest. Also had a a couple crashes within 5 minutes. They can happen at any time, when I'm on horse, running, fighting or not. Couldn't find any possible trigger, just that it crash gamescope too, I've attached the log.
[gamescope_crash.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14204414/gamescope_crash.log)
I also noticed a couple more people having Elder Ring crashing on their Steam Deck  recently:
https://www.protondb.com/app/1245620#JYSTFeo1_J
https://www.reddit.com/r/SteamDeck/comments/1ajvaql/proton_hotfix_updated_just_now_and_fixes_p3r/kp5j916/

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-1913639858

I am seeing this same issue on Pop_OS! I can only launch the game using Proton-7.0rc3-GE-1 but this only works for offline play as EAC doesn't start.

Can't launch the game since yesterday. It was working fine last week.

Kernel Version: 6.8.4-arch1-1
X Window Manager: KWin
Mesa 24.0.4-arch1.2

Well, the game launches just fine if you disable EAC (I renamed "eldenring.exe" to "start_protected_game.exe"). So something is wrong with EAC on Arch system at the moment.

**Edit**: 
I guess I found the culprit: lib32-amdvlk. I don't even know why it was installed since I use vulkan-radeon. 
Just installed lib32-vulkan-radeon and removed lib32-amdvlk and now the game launches.
I am also unable to launch the game on my Arch system. But on Windows it works fine.

> Well, the game launches just fine if you disable EAC (I renamed "eldenring.exe" to "start_protected_game.exe"). So something is wrong with EAC on Arch system at the moment.
> 
> **Edit**: I guess I found the culprit: lib32-amdvlk. I don't even know why it was installed since I use vulkan-radeon. Just installed lib32-vulkan-radeon and removed lib32-amdvlk and now the game launches.

I got lib32-amdvlk installed but I'm using Nvidia GPU, lib32-amdvlk seems to be the dependency of Steam client, if I try to uninstall it (archlinux), it uninstalls Steam including tens of other dependencies.

Out of curiosity, is the EAC/online working now on your computer? My EAC of Eledn Ring is never working, don't know if this is Nvidia's fault.
@c02y lib32-amdvlk is not a dependency of steam, a 32bit vulkan driver is. That probably means you didn't install the 32bit nvidia driver.
Yeah, it should be lib32-nvidia-utils

@mbriar Thanks.

I notice something, before I install `lib32-nvidia-utils`, uninstalling `lib32-amdvlk` will uninstall a lot of packages including steam, after I install `lib32-nvidia-utils`, uninstalling `lib32-amdvlk` only uninstall itself. Interesting.


AND big news, EAC is finally working, I'm so happy right now, the online function is working, don't need the `echo "%command%" | sed 's/start_protected_game.exe/eldenring.exe/' | sh` anymore, but I tried the hack for half an hour, found no one, seems not so many people are playing it right now, or my level 299 is too high, not sure. Anyway, thanks for this, multiplayer mode is finally working.

----------------------
*UPDATE:*
One hack succeeded when I'm writing this reply.

> Well, the game launches just fine if you disable EAC (I renamed "eldenring.exe" to "start_protected_game.exe"). So something is wrong with EAC on Arch system at the moment.
> 
> **Edit**: I guess I found the culprit: lib32-amdvlk. I don't even know why it was installed since I use vulkan-radeon. Just installed lib32-vulkan-radeon and removed lib32-amdvlk and now the game launches.

It works. 3q.

I have a strange issue with Elden Ring on Linux. Not with EAC. I backed up the two exe's and renamed a copy of elden_ring.exe to start_game_protected.exe to run the game unprotected and offline due to my use of mods.

My odd issue is that I play it modded with [Unlock the framerate](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/216), [Remove chromatic aberration](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/179),  [Fix the camera](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/118), [Adjust the FoV](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/325), [Increase animation distance](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/349), [Skip the intro logos](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/421/)﻿, and [Remove Black Bars](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/175) and load them with [Elden Mod Loader](https://www.nexusmods.com/eldenring/mods/117) with the launch parameter `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dinput8=n,b" %command%`. The mods work perfectly fine the first time but they don't work on subsequent launches of the game. It's like it quits accepting the WINEDLLOVERRIDES environment variable. To get it to launch with the mods again I can either reboot the system or change Proton versions. I'm closing the game with the in-game options, not using Alt+F4, pressing the Stop button in Steam, or Alt+Tab to close the window. 

I can change between Proton 9.0 and GE 9.5 over and over again and the mods keep working. If I don't change Proton versions the mods quit working. If I reboot and don't change the prefix, they start working. If I delete the Proton prefix the mods don't start working. Closing Steam and restarting it has no effect. Logging out and back in has no effect. 

Only rebooting and changing the Proton prefix version allows the mods to start working again.

Since I have a 3440x1440 144hz monitor those mods, which are the only ones that I use, are really essential to provide a better quality of life and better visuals for my ultrawide experience. The game plays a lot more smoother on CachyOS than it does on Windows 11, modded and unmodded, with the exact same in-game settings (all high with blur and dof off). It's so much smoother you'd think the developer's priority was Linux first, Windows second.

[System Information Gist](https://gist.github.com/rialpa84/985857a230df16ce3b0ef918af3933b5)

First run is switching from GE 9.5 to Proton 9.0, mods work
Second run is just running the game a 2nd time, mods don't work
Third run is deleting the game's compatdata folder and then running the game, mods don't work
Fourth run is switching from Proton 9.0 to GE 9.5, mods work again

[steam-1245620-first_run.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15516435/steam-1245620-first_run.log)
[steam-1245620-second_run.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15516438/steam-1245620-second_run.log)
[steam-1245620-third_run.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15516440/steam-1245620-third_run.log)
[steam-1245620-fourth_run.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15516443/steam-1245620-fourth_run.log)

To add, running winecfg in the prefix to set the dinput8 dll override there has the same effect as using the WINEDLLOVERIDES environment variable in the launch parameters. It only works the first time on a freshly changed prefix or after a reboot if the prefix's Proton version isn't changed. Luckily, the dll override set in winecfg survives when the prefix changes from one version to another. It doesn't survive if the prefix is deleted.

UPDATE

It was the Skip The Intro mod that was causing problems. Removing it made the rest of the mods work 100% of the time without needing to change prefixes or any other workarounds.
Hello @Tomjlennon, looking at your extended diagnostics information, Vulkan is broken in the Snap packaging you're using with Steam. You're seeing an NVIDIA-in-Snap packaging issue of some kind.
Thanks for identifying the root cause. I uninstalled Snap version of Steam and Installed Steam from another package that worked

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-2173110640

I've got the game working.

I originally had an nVidia RTX 3070 and replaced it with an AMD RX 7800 XT. I removed all packages relating to nvidia drivers but it seems that somewhere along the way I had removed a config file from /etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/

I found a template for the config file 10-amdgpu.conf in /usr/share/X11/xorg.conf.d/, I copied this over to the etc directory and rebooted.

It works great now.

@Dehir  According to their website, FromSoftware only provide support themselves to customers in Japan, all others must go through their region's publisher. I doubt Bandai Namco are going to trouble themselves with it, But I'll put in an email report anyway.

There are also reports from windows users at steam community site they are experiencing same error/issues.

issue_report_title: Power Rangers: Battle for the Grid (1110100)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Power Rangers: Battle for the Grid 
- Steam AppID of the game: 1110100

## System Information
Steam Deck

- Proton version: Proton 7.0-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms

Story mode cinematics replaced with SMPTE color bars. 

## Reproduction

Run the game, enter and play through story mode.

Despite game being marked Steam Deck verified, story mode cinematics represented as color bars. **Initial intro cinematic might be displayed correctly, but those which come further down the road aren't.**

I see the specific issue was addressed here #5881 and cinematics indeed are working on my Linux Desktop, but not on Deck.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Some of the lightning textures for the blue ranger's special charge do not render correctly, appearing as rectangles instead.

Changing some settings in the graphics menu changes other graphics settings as well.  The preset must be set to high or terrible quality textures are used.  Settings must carefully be customized to get everything at maximum.

Note I did not test on windows so this may be a game bug.

@Gibitwit Are you talking about ceonozoic blue? If that's the case, could you check in the move list for the name of the move? None of the moves I tried had broken graphical effects.

Also, please name what proton version you're using, and make a gist https://gist.github.com/ with your system information and link it here. You can get the information from steam by going to: help > system information.

Yes.

It was the second level Cenocharge, the lightning around the forearms was rectangles.  I am not getting the rectangles anymore.  My system did get a bunch of updates today, maybe one of those fixed something.

Proton experimental is used.

System information.  https://gist.github.com/Gibitwit/2ac5947df9c9f993203229efc1314607#file-gistfile1-txt
Note that some of it is wrong, but that is what Steam produced.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @jamincollins, please copy the contents of Steam Runtime Diagnostics from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `Steam Runtime Diagnostics`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.
https://gist.github.com/jamincollins/9e6312d3258348764daabe6357c6a347

Thanks, your system is configured to use AMDVLK (https://gist.github.com/jamincollins/9e6312d3258348764daabe6357c6a347#file-gistfile1-txt-L403-L419) and that's broken system wide making Vulkan unusable (https://gist.github.com/jamincollins/9e6312d3258348764daabe6357c6a347#file-gistfile1-txt-L84-L97, https://gist.github.com/jamincollins/9e6312d3258348764daabe6357c6a347#file-gistfile1-txt-L213-L225). ~~Report that driver issue to your distro's package maintainer(s) for AMDVLK and retest the game with mesa/RADV.~~
Looks like installing `vulkan-radeon` was enough.

Arena will not launch with the current Proton version (whatever the default happens to be if a forced compatibility isn't applied).   It does work if I force it to Proton 9.0-4.  The error that appears can be seen here:     

I'm running Debian Bookworm and the latest Steam client.

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Total War: Pharoah Dynasties
- Steam AppID of the game: 2951630

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU/PCIe/SSE2
- Video driver version: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 580.82.09
- Kernel version: 6.16.7-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [link](https://gist.github.com/DanZanzibar/4f0a9a681be06e73bb5becfbc7ad3ad2)
- Proton version: 1749140930 proton-9.0-4f

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-2951630.log.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22410237/steam-2951630.log.zip)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When in battles, frame rates drop to <5, presumably due to this shader compilation error that keeps showing up in the proton log:

```
88933.747:02fc:0300:warn:seh:OutputDebugStringW L"Direct3D failed to compile VS 'vs40_shadow_4_bones_dismember' in file 'FX/WeightedWithBlood.hlsl'\n\tfull path: 'FX/WeightedWithBlood.hlsl'\n\tError: error X3501: 'vs40_shadow_4_bones_dismember': entrypoint not found\n\n"
```

The menus and campaign map do not suffer from this issue at all.

Many proton versions have been tried, including GE-proton. Always the same issue.


## Reproduction
Start game, go into "Options", then "benchmark", then "battle benchmark".

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Pickup Basketball VR
- Steam AppID of the game: 1475200

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.1.4
- Kernel version: 5.13.1-178-tkg-bmq
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/a69ef95b1b481d585b42f7796cfb0204
- Proton version: 6.3-5, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

This game suffered a regression. Previously, it would work out of the box with Proton. By running it and reading steam-1475200.log, we saw that it needed api-ms-win-core-console-l1-2-0.dll.
@EsdrasTarsis obtained the stubfile offering the functions for this DLL from a wine [commit](https://source.winehq.org/git/wine.git/commit/24aba09015eb3ecbce99d148ac4b48cb173aba80) from Piotr Caban and recompiled in a build that I tested, but then another error appeared for library api-ms-win-core-kernel32-legacy-l1-1-5.dll. He also made a second build and the missing library this time would be api-ms-win-core-console-l3-2-0.dll. This one, however, is undocumented, so he had to go through some hoops and jumps to get the functions it provided, but he also made a third stub and a third build, which then worked.

This bug report thus will provide the solution to this problem. @EsdrasTarsis will upload his patch.

## Reproduction
Try and start the game with regular Proton 6.3-5 or Proton Experimental. It won't start.

No steam log on this case because the issue already has a solution.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @Patola, can you clarify if you think the regression was introduced by a game update or a Proton update? If it's a Proton update, then what's the most recent known good version of Proton that works with this game?

> Hello @Patola, can you clarify if you think the regression was introduced by a game update or a Proton update? If it's a Proton update, then what's the most recent known good version of Proton that works with this game?

It was a game update that added dependency on these DLLs. As I had the game uninstalled for a few weeks, I don't know exactly the timeframe and which specific update did that.

I am uploading the three patches:
0000 -- original Piotr Caban wine patch, taken straight from the link I provided (ms-win-core-console-l1-2-0)
0001 -- Esdras Tarsis' patch 1 for ms-win-core-console-l3-2-0
0002 -- Esdras Tarsis' patch 2 for ms-win-core-kernel32-legacy-l1-1-5

All the patches are explicitly named. For the sake of preserving signatures and credits, they are provided as is, and will need the parameter -F3 on the patch utility (fuzz 3) or even -F4 to be applied to the wine tree (in proton or otherwise).

After I finish compiling I will also provide my own proton build while this fix is not integrated.

[pickup-basketball-3-patches.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6821626/pickup-basketball-3-patches.tar.gz)

Ok, my proton build is up, I took the care to test it in a clean prefix before uploading: https://github.com/Patola/wine/releases/tag/6.3-pickupbasketball.

@kisak-valve I am not sure how these patches could get to proton, can you guide me? I am not the author; one author is Piotr Caban from the wine mailing list; the other author is @EsdrasTarsis. That's why I did not create the PR myself.

I think the ideal scenario would be to get the patches landed in upstream wine first, then you can make a pull request with the backported commits, a note to what it fixes, and the upstream commit IDs. That said, a Proton dev might read this issue report and handle the rest of the process on their own.

Esdras Tarsis' patches have been submitted to upstream:
[Patch 1 (api-ms-win-core-console-l3-2-0)](https://source.winehq.org/patches/data/209708)
[Patch 2 (api-ms-win-core-kernel32-legacy-l1-1-5)](https://source.winehq.org/patches/data/209709)

following_discussion.discussion_body:
UEVR injector for VR enablement

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8346.
@drjoms posted on 2024-12-26T15:12:40:

UEVR fails to detect game's process for injection.
See attachment for more information.
[uevr.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18253653/uevr.txt)

Just to clarify, this is likely not a specific game issue, but global injector issue. I did not try with other games, but I am pretty sure i will have same result with any game and this injector.
https://github.com/praydog/UEVR/releases
what it is:
https://youtu.be/J8Kk7coDGpk?t=133

Recently Epic Online Services updated when launching through Steam with Proton, it seems to have broken online compatibility with Epic friends. Reinstalled the game completely and started with a clean prefix and cloud saves off to ensure fresh install and it's still broken. Tried every troubleshooting step possible found on the net and I can surmise that the issue is related to the recent EOS update and no workaround currently. You are presented with the error 0x0002 'Epic Online Services could not be found, Please verify the install on your game client and relaunch Dead Island 2'

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b52ee4c1-c0c1-49ee-897c-453875bf9d1d)

Confirming this is broken on Steam Deck, Installed it today and I'm getting the above error 0x0002 Epic Online Services could not be found, which means i cant play multiplayer with friends. @kisak-valve would you mind helping me with how to report this to Valve? I'm unsure how to reach out to game developer or Valve on this because it works fine on Windows but not on Steam Deck

> Confirming this is broken on Steam Deck, Installed it today and I'm getting the above error 0x0002 Epic Online Services could not be found, which means i cant play multiplayer with friends. [@kisak-valve](https://github.com/kisak-valve) would you mind helping me with how to report this to Valve? I'm unsure how to reach out to game developer or Valve on this because it works fine on Windows but not on Steam Deck

If you wanna help by posting the log file here, then go to your steam library, click on the game, then the gear icon, then properties, then in general go down to the launch options box and paste this in "PROTON_LOG=1 %command%". Then play the game again and trigger the epic online services error. Then go into desktop mode, find the log file, then paste it here.

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/7667#issuecomment-2884350767

Log is from my Steam Deck

[steam-934700.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20231793/steam-934700.log)
This should hopefully be fixed in Proton Experimental ([bleeding-edge], https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Proton-Versions#proton-bleeding-edge).

> This should hopefully be fixed in Proton Experimental ([bleeding-edge], https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Proton-Versions#proton-bleeding-edge).

Epic Online Service is detected and it works again now on Steam Deck with Proton Experimental (bleeding-edge), Thank you so much!! 🎉🎉

[steam-934700.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21919476/steam-934700.log)

The issue might be back ?
Having the issue here too. I shared my log here

> The issue might be back ? Having the issue here too. I shared my log here

I'm not able to replicate any issue on both my Steam Deck or Desktop Arch Linux system w/ Proton Experimental (Bleeding-Edge and non-Bleeding-Edge)

Few questions:

1. Can you give more detail on precisely what you're seeing? Was it the same EOS error as mine previously? Screenshots may help as well
2. I could see in your log that you're running the game from a separate mounted disk, what is the the filesystem and what mount options are specified for that mount?
3. What are launch options (if any) in `Game Properties > General > Launch Options`
4. Do you have any custom environment variables set outside of steam? as in  `/etc/environment`?

Some suggestions:

1. Remove all custom launch options from `Game Properties > General > Launch Options`
2. Test in a fresh prefix (backup your old one first)
3. Check you DNS settings and perhaps temporarily set your dns on the machine to `1.1.1.1` (cloudflare) to ensure it's not a dns resolution issue.

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/7667#issuecomment-3212877028 I managed to make it work ! Thank you so much. Let me answer your questions, comments your suggestion one by one for anyone having the same issue: (TLDR: Proton bleeding edge fixed the issue) Answers 1. I am seeing this screen 2. The filesystem is extfat with these options -> nosuid,nodev,nofail,x-gvfs-show 3. I only had some options related to HDR and mangohud, removing them didn't help 4. No env variable outside of steam Trying your suggestion: 1. Didn't help 2. Didn't help 3. Didn't need to try. Steps for me to fix: going to steam, show tools, right click on proton experimental, beta tab, select bleeing edge, done I cannot make it work on non bleeding edge but it will do for now. Once again, thank you so much to @sh-jus

> (TLDR: Proton bleeding edge fixed the issue)
>
> 2. The filesystem is extfat with these options -> nosuid,nodev,nofail,x-gvfs-show

Glad you got it working!

Just a heads up though, exFAT might be a culprit here and could potentially cause issues down the road. Since it doesn't support hard/symbolic links (which Proton relies on), you could run into problems with prefixes not being able to properly link DOS drives to Linux paths, among other things. It might work fine now, but these issues can be tricky to diagnose later.

I'd recommend mounting your games on a drive formatted with ext4 (recommended) or btrfs instead. If you need cross-compatibility with Windows, the ntfs3 kernel driver could work as an alternative since it does support symlinks (unlike exFAT), but it has its own caveats. Just make sure to symlink your compatdata directory from your host filesystem to the NTFS drive if you go that route.

Upon careful verification I could confirm I mistook and all my drives were already formatted to ext4. I though it was one of the fossil from the era I needed Windows.
But this it a very useful advice and anyone reading this forum should consider this carefully.

Once again, thank you for your time !

issue_report_title: VRR locked at 60hz on Source 2013 games on Proton 9 and Experimental
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Source SDK 2013 Games (Tested: Team Fortress 2 Classic, Half-Life 2, Fortress Forever 2013 Branch)
- Steam AppID of the game: 220, 253530, 243750

## System Information
- GPU: RX 7600
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.1.0-devel (git-79c84e4b9c) [Also observed on other Mesa versions]
- Kernel version: 6.13.7 (also observed on earlier kernel versions)
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/webbongithub/1f6c957b248f919a2a9152ee41e0e9ad
- Proton version: 9.0-4, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game. [This affects many games and I haven't found this specific issue]
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

When Vsync is enabled on a VRR monitor, the FPS will never go above sixty. VRR /seems to work/ given it goes below 60 at times. This issue was observed on GNOME and KDE. Using Proton 8 or below resolves the issue.

<details>
<summary>Screenshots of the issue</summary>

## Proton 9
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4880b00-a042-4b25-9223-480e8f9ef006)

## Proton 8
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b49d0888-6d57-4b30-abbe-2224618641b9)
</details>

## Reproduction

1. Launch a Source 2013 game with a VRR monitor on Proton 9 or Experimental
2. Enable Vsync
3. Profit

following_discussion.discussion_body:
I looked around the dependencies for Proton that changed between version 8.0-5 and 9.0-4 . Of note, I saw that a commit in vkd3d-proton introduced a frame rate limiter. Commit is HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton@80f6c46 .

I then checked the most recent releases of Proton to see if the referenced code got introduced into Proton 9.0-4.

* All the commits in vkd3d-proton for Proton 9.0-4 :: https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/commits/4fd7d3ab3d35 - Frame limiter code shows up in the history.
* All the commits in vkd3d-proton for Proton 9.0-3 :: https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/commits/47840a4bb614  - Frame limiter code shows up in the history.
* All the commits in vkd3d-proton for Proton 9.0-2 :: https://github.com/HansKristian-Work/vkd3d-proton/commits/f6d39052afb1 - Frame limiter code does not show up in the history.

If possible, can you test the same game under Proton 9.0-2 to check if it's this commit causing the locked at 60hz issue?
@phora Do you know where I can find a binary version for 9.0-2? The registry used for building seems to be down/broken.

That I'm not entirely sure. I booted up my Steam Deck to check if it's possible to roll back to that specific version to test (which is no). However I did notice on both of the READMEs for vkd3d-proton and dxvk that there's these two environment variables you can set to uncap the frame rate.

Setting `VKD3D_FRAME_RATE` to 0 and `DXVK_FRAME_RATE` to 0 for the game should uncap the frame rate.

> That I'm not entirely sure. I booted up my Steam Deck to check if it's possible to roll back to that specific version to test (which is no). However I did notice on both of the READMEs for vkd3d-proton and dxvk that there's these two environment variables you can set to uncap the frame rate.
> 
> Setting `VKD3D_FRAME_RATE` to 0 and `DXVK_FRAME_RATE` to 0 for the game should uncap the frame rate.

Unfortunately setting those environment variables didn't work, still seeing the same behaviour.

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Sword Art Online: Integral Factor
- Steam AppID of the game: 2371630

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Vega 64
- Video driver version: 4.6 Mesa 23.1.6
- Kernel version: `6.4.12-1-MANJARO`
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [gist](https://gist.github.com/hasezoey/5d0240106768eafd05571b3f32e613a3)
- Proton version: 8.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-2371630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12466431/steam-2371630.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Unable to get into the game again, because of infinite loading of what (probably) should be a ingame browser window (according to some discussions, i dont know if that is correct)

## Reproduction

- start the game
- click to start the actual game ("Link Start")
- wait for the loading bar to finish
- observe "loading circle" still being there and text under the progress bar to read `Ready. Close the notice to start`
- wait some time
- observe nothing changing

Additional Context:
this issue seems to also happen to steam-deck users, but they seemingly can if it by moving the game from the sd-card to the internal storage

for me the game lives on a btrfs disk (including the compatdata)

also likely related to the "Ready. Close the notice to start" issue seems to be that the ingame shop seemingly never loads any data (which is the original reason for me to have restarted the game)

Note: i also tried not having a controller connected, in case this should change, but nothing changed

screenshot:
![Screenshot_20230829_174425](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/10911626/b4d3dccf-459e-47c9-b08e-c3f2a8c61565)
(Note the "X" in the top right does nothing when clicked-on)

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Hello @hasezoey, these look like some lines of interest from your Proton log:

```
warn:debugstr:OutputDebugStringA "[0829/173832.435:WARNING:gpu_process_host.cc(1279)] The GPU process has crashed 9 time(s)\n"
[...]
warn:debugstr:OutputDebugStringA "[0829/173832.436:FATAL:gpu_data_manager_impl_private.cc(447)] GPU process isn't usable. Goodbye.\n"
[...]
wine: Unhandled exception 0x80000003 in thread 350 at address 0000000182F50F00 (thread 0350), starting debugger...
```
Thread 0350 looks like libcef.dll.

also i forgot to mention in the original description is that current Proton Experimental has this game as a black-screen after window creation (only going back to 8.0-3 made it work)
but now trying to run with Proton Experimental again after the compatdata was created (and had a first start with the old version?) it now works in Proton Experimental
Experimental version: `1692722216 experimental-8.0-20230822`

[experimental steam-2371630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12467519/steam-2371630.log)

I have now tried to reinstall the game and noticed that what i mentioned about "a black screen on experimental", is actually on all tested proton versions(8.0-3 and Experimental) on the first start of the game and just requires to restart the game and it works.

After re-install i noticed that the initial menu with tabs `Summary` and `Analytics` dont load, but can be skipped by just hitting the `Agree` button (likely related to chromium).
Also after a re-install, on the first load (i dont know why, the images themself are loaded) the chromium window is layered over the loading screen images and so can be skipped by pressing `ESC` (or hitting a random corner, i am not entirely sure), but any start afterwards has the window behind the loading screen images (if it even is there).
Also this issue can be reproduced without having to fully go into the game and just going to `Menu -> Notifications` (which *can* be exited with `ESC`), which after pressing `ESC` shows for a brief second a "white window" (likely chromium)

Update:
via a steam discussion i have opened, i found that `GE-Proton8-14` works perfectly fine, here is how the loading screen is meant to look after load:
![Screenshot_20230905_140652](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/10911626/28f87655-9aca-4628-b885-e50a6a9d5678)
I'm glad my advice via the Steam discussion helped and yes, I'm `raposasemalma'.

issue_report_title: All Games Fail to Launch Through Protons Version >7
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: All Games, a log of Super Monkey Ball Banana Mania is presented as an example
- Steam AppID of the game: All, 1316910 is presented as an example

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1080 Mobile
- Video driver version: 560.28.03
- Native Vulkan Version: 1.3.283
- Mesa Version: 21.1.4
- Kernel version: 6.8.12
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [System Info](https://gist.github.com/E-Allie/eda219e1ec04c885b5285cc33c716df4)
- Proton version: >7

## I confirm:
- [✓] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [✓] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

[steam-1316910-NOTWORKING9.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16503793/steam-1316910-NOTWORKING9.log)
[steam-1316910-WORKING7.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16503794/steam-1316910-WORKING7.log)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
On all proton versions greater than 7, all Windows games fail to launch. A black window opens up for a brief moment, and then closes

When going through the log, I found that this could be the primary error/point of divergence between Proton 7 [working], and those greater than that, found on line 5566:

`83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault code=c0000005 flags=0 addr=(nil) ip=0 tid=01c0
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  info[0]=0000000000000008
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  info[1]=0000000000000000
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  rax=000000000000008f rbx=00000001222fe1a0 rcx=0000000000000000 rdx=0000000000000002
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  rsi=00000001222fe1b0 rdi=00005555931ceda0 rbp=00000001222fa130 rsp=00000001222fa118
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault   r8=0000000000000002  r9=0000000000000092 r10=0000000000000000 r11=000000000000007f
83586.114:0124:01c0:trace:seh:handle_syscall_fault  r12=00000001222fe1b0 r13=00005555931ceda0 r14=0000000000000223 r15=00000001222fa140
83586.114:0124:01c0:warn:seh:handle_syscall_fault backtrace: --- Exception 0xc0000005 at (nil):.
83586.114:0124:01c0:warn:seh:dump_syscall_fault backtrace: returning to user mode ip=00006ffff950e60a ret=c0000005
83586.114:0124:01c0:err:msvcrt:_wassert (L"!status && \"vkGetPhysicalDeviceSurfaceCapabilitiesKHR\"",L"../src-wine/dlls/winevulkan/loader_thunks.c",5115)`

The comparable line in the working log is 2779


## Reproduction

Freshly install Nvidia drivers, steam and proton.


<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Also, please copy the contents of `Help` > `Steam Runtime Diagnostics` to
   the gist.
6. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

With Proton Experimental, the game starts and runs good so far (have completed the Tutorial and got to the first city port).

One issue I have is that the game says Dualsense controller supported, however when the controller is connected wireless via Bluetooth, it is not recognized / not working in-game. This is both with Steam Input enabled or disabled.

But when connected wired via USB, then it works (button icons are OK).

EDIT1:
Using the Dualsense controller via Bluetooth, with Steam Input disabled and changing the registry:
DisableHidraw=1
Enable SDL=0

Results in Wine using the controller as Xinput in Wine / Control Panel / Game Controllers and the game then detects the controller as Xbox and works, the button icons are Xbox styled.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
anyone else getting degrading performance over time ?
The game is very fluid at first but as time goes on it gets choppier and choppier when moving the camera.

Kernel Version: 6.11.4-zen1-1-zen
Driver: AMD AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (radeonsi, navi32, LLVM 18.1.8, DRM 3.59, 6.11.4-zen1-1-zen)
Driver Version: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 24.2.5-arch1.1

> anyone else getting degrading performance over time ? The game is very fluid at first but as time goes on it gets choppier and choppier when moving the camera.
> 
> Kernel Version: 6.11.4-zen1-1-zen Driver: AMD AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (radeonsi, navi32, LLVM 18.1.8, DRM 3.59, 6.11.4-zen1-1-zen) Driver Version: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 24.2.5-arch1.1

Same on [my system](https://gist.github.com/douglascdev/ddbe6720e1be2faa8816687ae95dc36f). It gets unplayable with constant stutters and so far only restarting the game seems to fix it.

I'm also having my character get stuck like on [this protondb report](https://www.protondb.com/app/2429640#rMBN799pTP).

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8147#issuecomment-2489425828

I found disabling directX 12 helps a lot.

> I found disabling directX 12 helps a lot.

I'm already using dx11 but the kind of stutters I got seem pretty similar to what I got by turning on dx12. Probably unrelated tho, idk.

I tried using the default latest kernel on arch since @dlicois was using zen like me, just in case it was something like a weird kernel scheduling thing or something like that, but it changed nothing for me, both problems persisted.
Still gotta test for a bit longer, but I tried re-enabling the steam overlay and it seems like it stopped happening... @dlicois is yours disabled? Try turning it back on.
It has always been on for me. Perhaps you've benefited from the Mesa 24.3 release ?

> It has always been on for me. Perhaps you've benefited from the Mesa 24.3 release ?

I haven't updated yet, I'm on 24.2.7

Haven't gotten the fps stutters anymore(since re-enabling steam overlay), but since the last game update the player position rollbacks have gotten a lot more frequent, sometimes occurring right after starting to play.

EDIT: might actually be related to my internet this time, just noticed I'm having a lot of packet loss

The game still lose performance over time and it always starts with mouse movement for me. After a couple of hours, it turns to constant stutters every few seconds. Restart solves it. Enabling/disabling overlay didn't do much for me, well, overlay isn't even working for me right now so I'm not sure what else I can provide. This is on NVIDIA GPU.

There appears to be an input issue in Proton Experimental and 10+.  If you left click at the same time as pressing any of the movement keys, a/w/s/d it's as if the key input is not registered and the character does not move. Swapping to Proton 9.0-4 fixes the issue. Sysinfo: https://gist.github.com/wescode/eacb57c97befc2e710cc5b1e7237e2ae

Hello @wescode, please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options, reproduce the regression, and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.)

> Hello [@wescode](https://github.com/wescode), please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options, reproduce the regression, and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.)

[steam-2429640.log.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20739333/steam-2429640.log.gz)

@wescode couldn't reproduce your issue on proton 10 or experimental, could you give more details about in what situations it happens? do you have to be in battle? does classic or action mode matter?

I tried holding the left click and then, as I kept holding it, using wasd. Also tried activating both at the same time, but neither prevented movement.

> [@wescode](https://github.com/wescode) couldn't reproduce your issue on proton 10 or experimental, could you give more details about in what situations it happens? do you have to be in battle? does classic or action mode matter?
> 
> I tried holding the left click and then, as I kept holding it, using wasd. Also tried activating both at the same time, but neither prevented movement.

I'm using action mode. Sometimes it can take several attempts of pressing lmb+movement key. I also noticed that if I turn off the hud with alt+b I can't reproduce the issue. Once I turn the hud back on I can produce the issue.
@wescode in my case I had the hud on the whole time. Tried cycling through fullscreen, borderless and windowed options but neither of these helped me repro this issue

@wescode Are you clicking on something specific when you are simultaneously trying to move?  I also could not reproduce, and I found that I never had any reason to hold the left button down in my limited testing, so I'm wondering if there is some action where holding the left cursor causes the failure, or if you can reproduce when just running around randomly and holding down the left cursor button while not selecting anything.  Also - you are using Gnome+xorg as your window manager, right?

> [@wescode](https://github.com/wescode) Are you clicking on something specific when you are simultaneously trying to move? I also could not reproduce, and I found that I never had any reason to hold the left button down in my limited testing, so I'm wondering if there is some action where holding the left cursor causes the failure, or if you can reproduce when just running around randomly and holding down the left cursor button while not selecting anything. Also - you are using Gnome+xorg as your window manager, right?

I don't hold lmb, I click it the same time as a movement direction. I've tested it when clicking the test dummies or just clicking on the open ground, can happen either way. I'm actually running Gnome + Wayland.

@wescode Thank you for the additional details ... I was finally able to reproduce, which is the first step towards figuring out what is going wrong :)  In my testing so far, I could only reproduce with Gnome+Wayland.  It is extremely intermittent for me, so if you happen to have time to try Gnome+Xorg to see if you can reproduce there, please let me know.  In any case, we'll look into this further - thank you for the report!

> [@wescode](https://github.com/wescode) Thank you for the additional details ... I was finally able to reproduce, which is the first step towards figuring out what is going wrong :) In my testing so far, I could only reproduce with Gnome+Wayland. It is extremely intermittent for me, so if you happen to have time to try Gnome+Xorg to see if you can reproduce there, please let me know. In any case, we'll look into this further - thank you for the report!

@alasky17 Was the root cause every found? I actually tried out the latest GE-Proton and enabled Wayland and that seems to have fixed the issue.

@wescode Sadly not yet - it is still an open issue.  These bugs can be extremely tricky to track down :(  This seems like almost certainly a race condition since it is intermittent, so it makes sense that any change of variables could make it so rare that it basically never happens.
Yea interestingly enough I tried proton experimental today and I was not able to reproduce the issue now.

And now the issue is back. I'm not sure if it's related to some system update or what exactly. I will say that when this occurs I  also  have the issue where pressing the default ALT key  to show the cursor in action mode does not work. Both issues seem related and I didn't realize this until it was working the past few days and then stopped. No change in proton versions as I was using the latest GE-Proton the past few days. 

Edit: The issue where pressing ALT in action mode does not show the cursor also happens in proton experimental/proton 9

Edit2: So I noticed something interesting with the ALT show cursor bug. If I hold ALT then left click once the cursor will show, I can do the same to hide it again. To add a little more info to this, if I use GE-Proton and enable wayland both the show/hide cursor issue and original bug do not occur.

Hey @alasky17 I just wanted to let you know that the root cause of  both of my issues  appears to be ibus. An easy workaround is to kill the ibus-daemon and  both issues are then resolved; the  mouse shows when i hit ALT and there is no input issue when moving and clicking LMB. I will open an issue with ibus.
